Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the inventory.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the product is active in the store.

TaxType.title: TaxType
TaxType.description: Represents a tax type applicable to products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the tax type.

name.description: Name of the tax type (e.g., VAT, Sales Tax).

rate.description: Tax rate as a decimal (e.g., 0.10 for 10%).

InventoryMovement.title: InventoryMovement
InventoryMovement.description: Represents a movement of inventory (addition or removal).

id.description: Unique identifier for the inventory movement.

quantity.description: Quantity of the product added or removed (positive or negative).

movementDate.description: Date and time of the inventory movement.

type.description: The type of inventory movement.  For example 'inbound' or 'outbound'

id.description: Unique identifier for the sale transaction.

saleDate.description: Date and time of the sale.

SaleItem.title: SaleItem
SaleItem.description: Represents an item within a sale transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the sale item.

unitPrice.description: Price of the product at the time of the sale.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Represents a purchase order to replenish inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the purchase order.

purchaseDate.description: Date and time of the purchase order.

totalAmount.description: Total amount of the purchase order.

PurchaseItem.title: PurchaseItem
PurchaseItem.description: Represents an item within a purchase order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the purchase item.

unitPrice.description: Price of the product at the time of the purchase.

StoreConfiguration.title: StoreConfiguration
StoreConfiguration.description: Represents the configuration settings for the store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the store configuration.

securityPin.description: Security PIN to protect access to the system.

ticketConfiguration.description: JSON to store ticket configuration, such as logo, additional text, etc.

firestore.reasoning:
Based on the provided error and the application's requirements, the user is experiencing a 'Missing or insufficient permissions' error while trying to `list` documents in the `/stores/test-store/families` collection. This indicates a problem with the Firestore Security Rules. To address this, and following the core design principles, the following structure is recommended:

1.  **Authorization Independence:** The structure uses path-based ownership and denormalization to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. Specifically, the proposed structure assumes that the store and its associated data (families, products, tax types, etc.) are owned by a user or organization. We will store the `ownerId` of the store within the store document itself and any subcollections as required.

2.  **Structural Segregation:** The structure separates data with different access requirements into distinct collections. User-specific data resides under `/users/{userId}`, and store-specific data resides under `/stores/{storeId}`.

3.  **Access Modeling:**
    *   **Ownership:** Stores are owned by users, so the `ownerId` is crucial for access control. Subcollections like `families`, `products`, etc. will inherit this ownership by storing the `ownerId` within each document, enabling authorization independence.
    *   **Global Roles:** If the application requires admin roles, a dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection will be used.

4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure enables secure `list` operations by ensuring that all documents within a collection share the same security requirements. The `ownerId` field in the subcollections ensures that only the store owner can list the documents.  There is no mixing of access controlled documents within a collection.

5.  **Invariants:** The structure supports invariants like ownership and timestamps by enforcing them through security rules.

With respect to the error, the data structure is set up to support filtering of data via `ownerId` instead of the `get()` which breaks atomic operations. This ensures that security rules are easier to debug and maintain.
